text: Abstract. We describe the ECCO v4 non-linear inverse modeling framework and its baseline solution for the evolving ocean state between 1992 and 2011. Both are publicly available and subject to frequent automated regression testing. The modeling framework consists of a set of global conformal grids, a global model setup, data constraints and control parameters, the algorithmic differentiation interface, and a Matlab toolbox that is fully functional and grid-independent. We define an ocean state estimate to be a dynamically consistent solution to the Navier-Stokes equations that has no unaccounted for sources of heat and mass. Our baseline ECCO v4 solution is readily reproducible by any interested user. We find the baseline solution to be an acceptable model of the data and physical behavior in many respects, as is detailed here and in related publications. In addition, we have enabled users to self-assessess the ECCO v4 model to data mismatches. Our synergy of the ECCO v4 modeling framework and its baseline solution for the evolving ocean state is achieved through including the inverse estimate of the parameterized physical processes in the ECCO model setup. This way the new parameterized physics is made available for the general community to use. We provide a first assessment of relative importance of the external, parametric and structural model errors. We find the parametric and external errors to be of similar magnitude and dominate over the structural errors. We also confirm the importance of including the turbulent transport parameters in the inverse solution.
explanation: The text is highly domain-specific and reads like a genuine scientific abstract, with plausible terminology and references to ECCO v4 and ocean modeling. A minor typographical error ('assessess') also suggests human authorship rather than typical AI-generated text.
